name: Hungarian Stuffed Cabbage Rolls
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Freshly Rendered Lard or Oil
1 cup Seeded & Finely Chopped Anaheim Peppers or Unseeded Chopped Cubanelle Peppers
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Note: Paprika gravy recipe is included in this collection.
2. Heat the lard or oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and onion and cook until tender.
3. Stir in peppers and cook 1 minute more. Add rice and 1 cup chicken stock; stir to coat. Cook, stirring occasionally, about 5 minutes or until liquid is absorbed.
4. Stir in paprika, salt, and pepper; stir in pork mixture until well blended. Set aside to cool.
5. Core cabbage leaves and carefully remove them from the head of cabbage, keeping them as whole as possible. Blanch leaves in a large pot of salted water for 2 minutes or until pliable. Drain and allow to cool.
6. In a Dutch oven, spread 2 cups drained sauerkraut on the bottom of the pot; set aside.
7. In a large bowl, mix together ground pork, cooled rice mixture, egg, salt, and pepper.
8. Place cabbage leaves on the countertop and roll up into cylinders, folding in sides as you roll. Continue with remaining leaves. It may be necessary to piece together two leaves to make nice rolls.
9. Arrange the rolls on top of the sauerkraut in the pot. Spread pork slices over the rolls; spread remaining 1 cup sauerkraut evenly over all. Pour in remaining 1 1/2 cups chicken stock. The liquid should just cover cabbage rolls; if not, add more stock.
10. Bring to a simmer and cook for 1 hour 15 minutes.
11. This dish may be served with paprika gravy on the side.
